skills.name: code-review-discipline
skills.description: Use when requesting or receiving code review — dispatch reviewers, handle feedback rigorously, avoid performative agreement.

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.description: Use for 2+ independent tasks with no shared state or sequential dependency.

skills.name: engineering-docs
skills.description: Use when capturing a solved problem as categorized documentation with YAML frontmatter for fast lookup

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Execute a written implementation plan in a separate session with review checkpoints.

skills.name: file-todos
skills.description: This skill should be used when managing the file-based todo tracking system in the todos/ directory. It provides workflows for creating todos, managing status and dependencies, conducting triage, and integrating with slash commands and code review processes.

skills.name: galiana
skills.description: Show discipline analytics — defect escape rate, override rate, cost metrics, and agent scorecard

skills.name: interop-with-gsd
skills.description: If gsd-plugin is not installed, this skill is informational only. Use when the user mentions /gsd:* commands, references the GSD framework (Get Stuff Done), or asks how Clavain compares to spec-driven development workflows.

skills.name: interop-with-superpowers
skills.description: If superpowers is not installed, this skill is informational only. Use when the user mentions /superpowers:* commands or asks how Clavain relates to superpowers (obra/superpowers).

skills.name: interserve-engine
skills.description: Invoked from /clavain:interserve — dispatches tasks to Codex CLI agents (megaprompt or parallel), with debate triggers and Oracle escalation. Internal; users invoke via slash command.

skills.name: landing-a-change
skills.description: Use when implementation is complete and tests pass — verify, document, and land on trunk.

skills.name: lane
skills.description: Manage thematic work lanes — discover, create, tag beads, show velocity and starvation scores

skills.name: project-onboard
skills.description: Use to set up any Sylveste project (new or existing) — introspects, creates repo, scaffolds docs/automation/observability, seeds via interpath.

skills.name: refactor-safely
skills.description: Use for significant refactoring — duplication detection, characterization tests, staged execution, simplicity review.

skills.name: ship
skills.description: Use when work is done and you need to commit, push, close beads, and sync in one shot. The quick session-close ceremony.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

skills.name: upstream-sync-engine
skills.description: Invoked from /clavain:upstream-sync — checks beads/oracle/superpowers/compound for updates, generates upgrade checklist. Internal.

skills.name: using-clavain
skills.description: Use at start of any conversation — how to find/use skills, agents, commands. Requires Skill invocation before ANY response, including clarifying questions.

skills.name: using-tmux-for-interactive-commands
skills.description: Use for interactive CLI tools (vim, git rebase -i, REPLs) that need a real terminal — drives tmux detached sessions via send-keys/capture-pane.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

agents.name: data-migration-expert
agents.description: Validates data migrations, backfills, and production data transformations against reality. Use when PRs involve ID mappings, column renames, enum conversions, or schema changes.

agents.name: plan-reviewer
agents.description: Use this agent when a major project step has been completed and needs to be reviewed against the original plan and coding standards. Examples: <example>Context: The user is creating a code-review agent that should be called after a logical chunk of code is written. user: "I've finished implementing the user authentication system as outlined in step 3 of our plan" assistant: "Great work! Now let me use the plan-reviewer agent to review the implementation against our plan and coding standards" <commentary>Since a major project step has been completed, use the plan-reviewer agent to validate the work against the plan and identify any issues.</commentary></example> <example>Context: User has completed a significant feature implementation. user: "The API endpoints for the task management system are now complete - that covers step 2 from our architecture document" assistant: "Excellent! Let me have the plan-reviewer agent examine this implementation to ensure it aligns with our plan and follows best practices" <commentary>A numbered step from the planning document has been completed, so the plan-reviewer agent should review the work.</commentary></example>

agents.name: bug-reproduction-validator
agents.description: Systematically reproduces and validates bug reports to confirm whether reported behavior is an actual bug. Use when you receive a bug report or issue that needs verification.

agents.name: codex-delegate
agents.description: Delegates well-scoped tasks to Codex CLI for cost-efficient execution. Use proactively for implementation, exploration, search, test generation, and code review when the task has clear scope and success criteria. Keep architecture, brainstorming, and interactive work in Claude.

agents.name: pr-comment-resolver
agents.description: Addresses PR review comments by implementing requested changes and reporting resolutions. Use when code review feedback needs to be resolved with code changes.

agents.name: ui-polish
agents.description: Iterates on UI refinements — tooltips, labels, layouts, mobile fixes, spacing, chart styling. Takes a screenshot or URL + natural language instruction and makes targeted frontend edits. Use when the user provides visual feedback about a web or TUI interface.

commands.name: bead-sweep
commands.description: Scan open beads for stale trackers already implemented — deterministic checks then LLM verification.

commands.name: brainstorm
commands.description: Collaborative brainstorm — assess clarity, research repo, explore approaches, capture design. Auto-handoff to /write-plan.

commands.name: campaign
commands.description: Orchestrate epic execution — read children, topo-sort by dependencies, dispatch phases sequentially with parallel features

commands.name: changelog
commands.description: Create engaging changelogs for recent merges to main branch

commands.name: clavain-doctor
commands.description: Quick health check — verifies MCP servers, external tools, beads, and plugin configuration without making changes

commands.name: clavain-help
commands.description: Show Clavain commands organized by daily drivers first, then by workflow stage

commands.name: clavain-init
commands.description: Scaffold .clavain/ agent memory directory in the current project

commands.name: clavain-review
commands.description: Perform exhaustive code reviews using multi-agent analysis and deep inspection

commands.name: clavain-status
commands.description: Unified status view across Clavain, artifact generation, doc drift, and coordination

commands.name: clodex-toggle
commands.description: Toggle interserve execution mode

commands.name: codex-bootstrap
commands.description: Bootstrap or check Clavain Codex installation health (install + wrapper sync + doctor)

commands.name: compound
commands.description: Document a recently solved problem to compound your team's knowledge

commands.name: create-agent-skill
commands.description: Create or edit Claude Code skills with expert guidance on structure and best practices

commands.name: debate
commands.description: Run a structured Claude↔Codex debate before implementing a complex task

commands.name: describe-pr
commands.description: Generate PR title and description from current branch commits

commands.name: distill
commands.description: Synthesize accumulated docs into categorized solutions and generate missing SKILL-compact.md files

commands.name: execute-plan
commands.description: Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints

commands.name: fixbuild
commands.description: Run build, capture error, fix it, re-run — fast loop for type errors and build failures without heavyweight debugging

commands.name: galiana
commands.description: Discipline analytics — view KPIs, report defects, or reset cache

commands.name: generate-command
commands.description: Create a new custom slash command following conventions and best practices

commands.name: handoff
commands.description: Generate a concise session handoff summary for pasting into a new conversation

commands.name: heal-skill
commands.description: Fix incorrect SKILL.md files when a skill has wrong instructions or outdated API references

commands.name: interserve
commands.description: Run Codex-first execution flow for larger scope work

commands.name: land
commands.description: Run the landing workflow for trunk-based handoff

commands.name: migration-safety
commands.description: Orchestrate database migration and data-risk work with safety checks (integrity, migration, deployment).

commands.name: model-routing
commands.description: Toggle subagent model routing between economy (smart defaults) and quality (all opus) mode

commands.name: peers
commands.description: Read-only viewer for detected peer agent rigs (superpowers, GSD, compound-engineering). Lists detection state and recommended bridge skills. Never makes changes.

commands.name: plan-review
commands.description: Have multiple specialized agents review a plan in parallel

commands.name: pr-triage
commands.description: Triage all open PRs — batch by theme, review with parallel agents, generate report, walk through decisions

commands.name: quality-gates
commands.description: Gate orchestrator — prepares diff, delegates review to flux-drive, enforces pass/fail gate

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Search across all knowledge systems — curated docs, memory files, legacy entries

commands.name: refactor
commands.description: Plan and execute safe refactors with explicit risk controls

commands.name: reflect
commands.description: Capture sprint learnings and advance from reflect to done

commands.name: repro-first-debugging
commands.description: Disciplined bug investigation — reproduce first, then diagnose. Composes debugging agents.

commands.name: resolve
commands.description: Resolve findings from any source — auto-detects TODOs, PR comments, or todo files, then resolves in parallel

commands.name: review-discipline
commands.description: Run disciplined code review and feedback triage

commands.name: review-doc
commands.description: Quick single-pass document refinement — assess clarity, score quality, fix issues, offer iteration

commands.name: route
commands.description: Universal entry point — discovers work, resumes sprints, classifies tasks, and dispatches to /sprint or /work

commands.name: setup
commands.description: Bootstrap Clavain for the active runtime (Codex or Claude Code) and verify health

commands.name: smoke-test
commands.description: Run smoke tests against a running app — detect dev server, walk critical user journeys, report results

commands.name: sprint
commands.description: Phase sequencer — brainstorm, strategize, plan, execute, review, ship. Use /route for smart dispatch.

commands.name: sprint-dag
commands.description: Visualize sprint execution as a DAG — shows phases, dispatches, and artifacts

commands.name: sprint-status
commands.description: Deep sprint workflow scan — session continuity, pipeline, plan, beads, recommendations.

commands.name: strategy
commands.description: Structure brainstorm output into a PRD with features, create beads for tracking, and validate before detailed planning

commands.name: tdd
commands.description: Run disciplined test-driven development (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) for a task

commands.name: todos
commands.description: Create and maintain file-based todo tracking

commands.name: triage
commands.description: Triage and categorize findings for the CLI todo system

commands.name: update-check
commands.description: Conservative update check — reports available Sylveste/Clavain/Codex updates without changing anything

commands.name: upstream-sync
commands.description: Check upstream repos (beads, oracle, superpowers, compound-engineering) for updates and generate upgrade checklist

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Run completion verification before claiming work is done

commands.name: work
commands.description: Execute work plans efficiently while maintaining quality and finishing features

commands.name: write-plan
commands.description: Create detailed implementation plan with bite-sized tasks
